ogTitle: Higher Macdonald vs Hill End: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Higher Macdonald and Hill End.
heading: Higher Macdonald vs Hill End — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Higher Macdonald, New South Wales and Hill End, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hill End has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Higher Macdonald (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Higher Macdonald or Hill End safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Higher Macdonald has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Hill End scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Higher Macdonald scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Hill End scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Higher Macdonald or Hill End?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hill End has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
